AMD.reasoning: CATALYST WINDOW: Q1 2026 earnings on May 5, 2026 — approximately 4 trading days away. This is the primary binary catalyst. Consensus EPS $1.18, revenue ~$9.8B (+32% YoY). Critically, AMD already pre-guided Q1 softly after the Q4 2025 beat ($1.53 EPS vs $1.24 est.), meaning the bar is set conservatively — a beat is more probable than a miss. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Positive. The Meta $60B multi-year infrastructure deal (AMD GPUs + Venice CPUs + custom silicon co-engineering) provides Data Center revenue visibility that analysts call transformational. TSMC 2nm first-mover confirmed April 15 by CEO Lisa Su — ahead of both Nvidia and Intel on next process node. Goldman Sachs top pick heading into earnings; Evercore ISI $358 bull target, Melius Research $380; 39 Buy / 0 Sell among 51 covering analysts. Headwinds: 25% tariff on advanced AI chips (MI325X/H200) is an ongoing margin headwind; Nvidia retains ~89% server GPU share limiting AMD's TAM. On balance, news flow is clearly positive. INSIDER ACTIVITY: Net selling — CEO Lisa Su sold ~$16M in March 2026; board-level distribution is 188 sells vs 83 buys (2:1 sellers). This is a bearish lean but is not historically a short-term catalyst signal and does not override the bullish fundamental thesis. TECHNICAL SETUP GOING IN: Extended but trend is intact. RSI 14 at 82.09, RSI 21 at 85.78 — significantly overbought on daily. Daily ADX at 73.57 with Plus DI 56.62 vs Minus DI 10.51 = strong trending market. EMA fully aligned uptrend. Price is 47% above 50 SMA and up ~72% over 21 days (parabolic April run). Z-score 50 at 2.47. These readings normally warrant caution, but pre-earnings compression of uncertainty can push overbought names higher before the event. Hourly signals are slightly mixed: hourly MACD histogram turned negative (-1.69) and hourly minus_di (33.53) exceeds plus_di (20.45), suggesting short-term intraday consolidation / pullback — not trend reversal. Hourly support confirmed at $328.81 (6 tests, volume-confirmed). Nearest hourly resistance at $352.99 (prior session high). Entry rationale: current price (~$337-345) is constructive within the pre-earnings 4-day window. The lowered Q1 guidance already baked in, combined with strong Data Center demand signals from Meta deal, makes a positive surprise likely. Target sized at ~8% above entry (analyst day / earnings magnitude), stop at pre-catalyst hourly support. Risk: stock is already near Evercore's $358 bull target — much of the good news is priced in. A beat-and-lower for Q2 guidance (tariff headwinds, competitive pressure) could trigger a sell-the-news response despite a headline beat. Confidence capped at 62 due to extended valuation and insider distribution overhang.
